records.ety: [OE. desclosen, disclosen, fr. disclos, desclos, not shut in, open, OF. desclos, p. p. of desclore to open, F. dclore; pref. des- (L. dis-) + clore to shut, fr. L. claudere to shut. See Close, and cf. Disclusion.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Disclosed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Disclosing.]

defs: To unclose; to open; -- applied esp. to eggs in the sense of to hatch.
quotes: The ostrich layeth her eggs under sand, where the heat of the discloseth them.

defs: To remove a cover or envelope from;; to set free from inclosure; to uncover.
quotes: The shells being broken, . . . the stone included in them is thereby disclosed and set at liberty.

defs: To lay open or expose to view; to cause to appear; to bring to light; to reveal.
quotes: How softly on the Spanish shore she plays, Disclosing rock, and slope, and forest brown!
Her lively looks a sprightly mind disclose.

defs: To make known, as that which has been kept secret or hidden; to reveal; to expose; as, events have disclosed his designs.
quotes: If I disclose my passion, Our friendship 's an end.
